method: Within-policy ranking of native OS/process/filesystem binding candidates currently implemented in product TypeScript (raw bun:ffi / readlink / ad-hoc OS probing). Candidates qualify under scripts/check-rust-scope.ts (native bindings / OS-process-fs integration / measured hot paths) and follow docs/porting-to-natives.md. Speculative algorithmic TS->Rust ports are EXCLUDED unless they clear all six gates in docs/native-ffi-optimization-policy.md with profilerSelfTime evidence; none qualify today because the profiling corpus has zero profiler artifacts.

gateLegend.corpusEvidence: gate 1: profiling-corpus trace shows user-visible latency/RSS impact
gateLegend.selfTimeAttribution: gate 2: profilerSelfTime artifact (or fallback-toggle) attributes the cost
gateLegend.measuredFfiOverhead: gate 3: N-API/marshalling overhead measured vs JS/TS baseline
gateLegend.representativeWin: gate 4: representative p50/p95 win on realistic inputs
gateLegend.behaviorParity: gate 5: byte/behavior parity across rendered/persisted/provider/observable output
gateLegend.operationalCost: gate 6: fallback, packaging, and rollback cost documented

exclusions: Architectural JS-surface-shrink without a native-binding / OS-process-fs / measured-hot-path rationale (non-goal per the approved spec).
Algorithmic TS->Rust ports without a profilerSelfTime artifact (gate 2 currently unsatisfiable: docs/perf-profiling-corpus.md records zero captured profiler artifacts).

Closed cpu-hotspot-map.json backlog (H01-H11, M01-M05) unless a future PR clears gates 1-6 with fresh corpus evidence; see docs/hotspot-map-successor.md. Do not reopen it as an implementation backlog.
Already-native platform/system primitives (grep, fd/glob, text width/wrap/slice, highlight, html->md, countTokens, ast, ps/pty/shell, sixel, clipboard, xxHash, JSON.parse/stringify).
Implementation of FN03 (file-lock) and FN04 (which) in this sweep; both are listed as deferred candidates with a nextEvidenceAction.

candidates.rationale: Currently ad-hoc bun:ffi dlopen of kernel32 in product TS. Moving the OS-call boundary into pi-natives consolidates platform integration behind the sanctioned N-API surface and removes ad-hoc FFI from product code. This is a native-binding rationale, not a speculative perf port, so the FFI optimization gates (1-4) are not required.

corpusEvidence.note: native OS binding, not a leftover algorithmic hot path

selfTimeAttribution.note: no perf claim; consolidation of an OS boundary

measuredFfiOverhead.note: runs once at terminal setup/teardown, not in a hot loop

behaviorParity.note: console-mode enable/restore must reproduce prior behavior; non-fatal on failure; verified by win32 CI build/load/shape/no-throw + conditional restore

operationalCost.note: guarded lazy native lookup keeps TS usable if exports absent; rollback = revert callsites/PR; packaging via existing pi-natives addon

candidates.nextEvidenceAction: Add a Windows CI exercise in .github/workflows/ci.yml verifying build/load/export-shape/no-throw for enableWindowsVtInput and conditional restore via setConsoleInputMode when applied is true. Do not claim real console-mode bit toggling on windows-latest (GHA console may be non-interactive).

candidates.policyClass: native-binding: OS TTY/filesystem (Linux readlink /proc/self/fd/0; POSIX ttyname(3))
candidates.rationale: Currently Linux readlinkSync + bun:ffi dlopen of libSystem/libc ttyname in product TS. The OS TTY lookup belongs behind @gajae-code/natives; getTerminalId keeps its tmux/env fallback policy in TS.

corpusEvidence.note: native OS binding, not a leftover algorithmic hot path

behaviorParity.note: identical device-path string on Linux/macOS; null on win32/non-TTY/pipe; preserve the /dev/ prefix filter that getTerminalId depends on

operationalCost.note: guarded lazy native lookup returns null if export absent; rollback = revert callsites/PR

candidates.nextEvidenceAction: Add POSIX CI tests for ttyname/readlink parity and a cross-platform behavioral test asserting getTtyPath() returns null on non-TTY stdin on every CI platform.

candidates.rationale: Current TS directory-lock protocol (fs.mkdir lock dir + process.kill(pid,0) liveness) has a documented read->unlink race because POSIX has no atomic compare-and-delete for the directory protocol. A native primitive could expose platform locking/liveness while TS keeps stale/retry/audit policy.

behaviorParity.note: cross-platform lock semantics + stale-owner handling must be specified first

operationalCost.note: rollback/fallback for established directory-lock semantics must be documented

candidates.nextEvidenceAction: Write a follow-up design comparing flock/fcntl vs LockFileEx semantics, stale-owner handling, timeout behavior, and rollback; get owner sign-off before opening a native PR.

candidates.policyClass: native-binding: executable resolver / OS path & toolchain discovery
candidates.rationale: $which wraps Bun.which (already native for the normal path) but adds macOS Xcode/CLT discovery (reads $DEVELOPER_DIR, /var/db/xcode_select_link, scans toolchain usr/bin). A native export could consolidate platform executable resolution, but Bun.which already covers the common path, so priority is lower.

behaviorParity.note: must define exact PATH/PATHEXT/toolchain parity vs current TS + Bun.which

candidates.nextEvidenceAction: Capture concrete cases where Bun.which is insufficient and define exact PATH/PATHEXT/toolchain-discovery parity before proposing a native resolver PR.
